name: Byzantine Empire (Palaiologan banner, c.1261–1453)

colors.symbolism: the gold cross and the four fire-steels

description: A red field bearing a gold cross, with a gold fire-steel (a letter 'B' / beta) in each of the four quarters — the tetragrammic cross of the Palaiologos dynasty.
history: The gold cross with four 'B'-shaped fire-steels on red is the best-known flag associated with the Palaiologos dynasty (1261–1453), the last imperial house of the Byzantine Empire. The four devices are usually read as fire-steels or as the initial beta of an imperial motto ('King of Kings ruling over rulers'). A separate imperial banner bore a black double-headed eagle on gold.
